_comment: Analytes reports contain but the master sheet never did. A hand-kept sheet usually tracks one or two people's blood panels; a family's actual medical life is wider than that. Merged on top of data/analytes.json at load, never clobbered by re-importing the sheet.
_naming: The codebook is keyed by analyte name, so a urine RBC and a blood RBC cannot both be called 'RBC'. The urine ones are named 'Urine X' and carry 'X' as an alias: domain gating (section -> urine) then routes a printed 'RBC' to Urine RBC inside a urine section, and to the blood RBC everywhere else. Get this wrong and a dipstick reading lands in a blood cell count.
_ranges: No reference ranges here. The master sheet carries ranges only for the analytes it tracked; these are new and have none. Values are stored and queryable, but nothing is flagged normal/abnormal against a range we invented.
_urine_comment: Urine routine + microscopy. Mostly qualitative. Appears across several family members.
_echo_comment: Qualitative 2D-echo findings ('INTACT', 'TRIVIAL TR'). Narrative, not trendable -- but they make 'what did the last echo say' answerable.
_sleep_comment: BiPAP / CPAP therapy reports. An entire dimension of health the sheet had no concept of. 'Is the AHI improving?' becomes a query.
